mechanism_narrative: RSAFS (retail sales) trending upward signals robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS monthly change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p<0.10) over rolling 5-year windows, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 8%, it indicates severe labor market weakness that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, causing immediate negative reaction in equity markets.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return following UNRATE crossing above 8% is not negative and statistically different from zero (t-test p>0.05) over all occurrences, hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation remains elevated above 2% while the fed funds rate is below 1%, the negative real interest rate environment signals potential overheating and future tightening, which lowers equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from CPIAUCSL rising and FEDFUNDS below 1% yields a positive 60-day return in more than 40% of occurrences, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE > 8%), rising CPI inflation reduces real purchasing power without commensurate wage growth, creating stagflationary pressures that depress equity prices.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is not negative in at least 60% of months when UNRATE > 8% and CPIAUCSL rises sequentially, hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month consecutive decline in INDPRO signals contraction in industrial output, which precedes broader economic weakness and leads to lower corporate earnings, resulting in negative returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If the average 60-day return following two-month consecutive INDPRO declines is positive or not significantly negative (p>0.05), hypothesis is invalid.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST drops below 500,000 units, it indicates a severe contraction in the housing market, which has broad negative spillover effects on consumer wealth and construction employment, hurting equity markets over the medium term.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return is not negative in at least 60% of months when HOUST < 500, the hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deterioration in both employment (PAYEMS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad economic weakness that is particularly damaging to cyclical stocks, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint signal of declining PAYEMS and declining HOUST produces a positive 60-day return in more than 30% of instances over any 3-year period, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS below 0.5%), rising CPI inflation is interpreted as a signal of economic recovery and demand strength, which supports higher equity valuations over the short term.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL rises, hypothesis is falsified.
